file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP Smart Dashboard page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for the promotional page, footer links, subscription management, printer options, and enrollment features. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element management functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the HP Smart Dashboard page. Provides locators for signup buttons, promotional page elements, footer navigation links, subscription state indicators, printer management options, and enrollment links. This page object is used to interact with the main dashboard interface where users can manage their HP Instant Ink subscriptions, view printer status, and access various account features. Extends BasePageObject to leverage the elements attribute pattern for locator storage.

locators.ui_element_description: Sign up now button on the HP Smart dashboard that initiates the enrollment process for HP Instant Ink service. This is the primary call-to-action button for new users.
Background container element for the promotional page section. Uses a partial ID match to target the promotional page container with parity styling.
Disclaimer container on the promotional page that displays legal terms, conditions, or important notices related to the HP Instant Ink service.
Keypoint Intelligence link in the footnote section that provides additional information about ink savings and related intelligence data.
Shipping and billing management page container where users can update their shipping address and billing payment information for HP Instant Ink subscriptions.
Footer link that navigates to the main HP.com website. Located in the footer section of the dashboard page.
Footer link that navigates to the HP Wireless Print Center for printer setup and wireless printing configuration.
Footer link that navigates to the HP Help Center where users can find support articles, troubleshooting guides, and FAQs.
Footer link that navigates to the HP Instant Ink Terms of Use legal document page.
Footer link that navigates to the HP Privacy Statement page detailing how HP collects and uses customer data.
Footer link that navigates to the Your Privacy Choices page where users can manage their privacy preferences and opt-out options.
Subscription state indicator element that displays the current status of the HP Instant Ink subscription. Uses multiple selector options to match different page variations.
Enrollment link with text 'Enroll in HP Instant Ink' that initiates the HP Instant Ink enrollment process for users who have not yet subscribed.
Printer options control element that expands or collapses the printer management options menu. Uses aria-controls attribute to identify the expandable section.
Update plan link that navigates to the plan update page where users can change their HP Instant Ink subscription plan tier.
Remove printer link in the printer options menu that allows users to remove a printer from their HP Instant Ink account.
Printers page title heading element that displays the title of the printers management page section.
Print plans page container element that displays available HP Instant Ink subscription plans and promotional offers. Uses partial ID match for the promotional page container.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the DashboardHPSmartPage page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance and elements container. Then defines all dashboard page UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates locators for the signup button, promotional page elements (background and disclaimer), keypoint intelligence link, shipping/billing page, six footer navigation links (HP.com, Wireless Print Center, Help Center, Terms of Use, Privacy, Privacy Choices), subscription state indicator, enrollment link, printer options control, update plan link, remove printer link, printers page title, and print plans page container. All locators use data-testid attributes for reliability where available, with some using CSS class or ID selectors with partial matching for dynamic elements.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object follows the BasePageObject pattern where locators are stored as attributes on the self.elements object rather than as direct instance attributes. This is a different pattern from typical page objects but provides a namespace for element organization. The page contains 18 locators covering promotional content, footer navigation, subscription management, and printer options. No action methods are defined - this appears to be a locator-only page object where interaction methods may be inherited from BasePageObject or called directly on the locators. The locators use primarily data-testid attributes which are the most reliable selector strategy. Some locators use composite selectors with multiple options to handle page variations. The footer section has comprehensive coverage with 6 different footer links.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Dashboard Side Menu navigation component. Contains locators for all menu items and submenu items in the dashboard side navigation panel, and provides interaction methods for clicking menu items with automatic expansion handling for parent menus and mobile responsive layouts. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the dashboard side navigation menu. Provides locators for menu toggle button, main menu items (Instant Ink, Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking, My Account, Printers), and submenu items (Shipping & Billing, Notifications). Contains intelligent click methods that automatically handle parent menu expansion, mobile layout menu expansion, and retry logic for menu items that may be initially hidden. This page object handles the complexity of responsive menu behavior where menu items may be collapsed on mobile or nested under parent menu items.

locators.ui_element_description: Mobile hamburger menu toggle button that expands or collapses the side navigation menu on mobile and tablet layouts. Only visible on smaller screen sizes.
HP Instant Ink parent menu item in the side navigation. This is an expandable menu item that contains submenu items like Overview, Update Plan, Print History, and Shipment Tracking.
Overview submenu item under HP Instant Ink that navigates to the account overview page showing subscription status and printer information.
Update Plan submenu item under HP Instant Ink that navigates to the plan update page where users can change their subscription tier.
Print and Payment History submenu item under HP Instant Ink that navigates to the history page showing past print jobs and payment transactions.
Shipment Tracking submenu item under HP Instant Ink that navigates to the shipment tracking page showing ink cartridge delivery status.
My Account parent menu item in the side navigation. This is an expandable menu item that contains submenu items like Shipping & Billing and Notifications.
Shipping & Billing submenu item under My Account that navigates to the page where users can update shipping address and payment method.
Notifications submenu item under My Account that navigates to the notifications preferences page where users can manage email and communication settings.
Printers menu item in the side navigation that navigates to the printers management page where users can view and manage their registered printers.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the DashboardSideMenuPage page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance and elements container. Sets the default timeout for all page operations to 30000 milliseconds (30 seconds) using page.set_default_timeout(30000). Then defines all side menu UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates locators for the mobile menu toggle button, the HP Instant Ink parent menu item, five submenu items under Instant Ink (Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking), the My Account parent menu item, two submenu items under My Account (Shipping & Billing, Notifications), and the Printers menu item. All locators use data-testid attributes for reliable element identification.

parameters.name: parent_element
parameters.description: The parent menu element that needs to be expanded first

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that attempts to click a target submenu element with automatic parent menu expansion if needed. First tries to click the target_element directly. If that click fails (raises an exception, likely because the element is not visible or not clickable), it catches the exception, then clicks the parent_element to expand the parent menu, and then clicks the target_element again. This handles the common scenario where submenu items are hidden until their parent menu is expanded. The try-except block with bare except ensures the method continues even if the first click attempt fails for any reason.

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The submenu element under HP Instant Ink to click

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that attempts to click a submenu element under the HP Instant Ink menu with intelligent handling of mobile layout and parent menu expansion. First tries to click the target_element with a short 2000ms timeout and returns immediately if successful. If that fails, it catches the exception and continues. Then checks if the mobile menu toggle button (expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout) is visible, and if so, clicks it to expand the mobile menu and waits for the target_element to become visible with a 30000ms timeout. This handles mobile responsive layouts where the menu is initially collapsed. Both of these steps are wrapped in try-except blocks to handle cases where they are not needed. Finally, calls _click_with_parent_expansion to click the target element with automatic expansion of the instant_ink_menu_link parent if needed. This method handles three levels of menu complexity: direct click, mobile menu expansion, and parent menu expansion.

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The submenu element under My Account to click

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that clicks a submenu element under the My Account menu with automatic parent menu expansion. Simply calls _click_with_parent_expansion with the target_element and my_account_menu_link as the parent. This is a convenience wrapper that simplifies calling code by not requiring the caller to specify the parent menu link.

Clicks the Overview menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the account overview page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with overview_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method abstracts away the complexity of menu expansion logic and provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the overview page.

Clicks the Update Plan menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the plan update page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with update_plan_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the plan update page.

Clicks the Print and Payment History menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the print and payment history page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with print_history_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the history page.

Clicks the Shipment Tracking menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the shipment tracking page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with shipment_tracking_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the shipment tracking page.

Clicks the Shipping & Billing submenu item under My Account in the side navigation to navigate to the shipping and billing management page. Calls _click_with_account_expansion with shipping_billing_submenu_link to handle automatic expansion of the My Account parent menu if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the shipping and billing page.

Clicks the Notifications submenu item under My Account in the side navigation to navigate to the notifications preferences page. Calls _click_with_account_expansion with notifications_submenu_link to handle automatic expansion of the My Account parent menu if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the notifications page.

Expands the HP Instant Ink parent menu in the side navigation to reveal its submenu items. First checks if the overview_menu_link (a submenu item) is already visible. If not visible, it attempts to expand the mobile menu toggle if it is visible by clicking expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout with a 30000ms timeout, wrapped in a try-except block to handle cases where mobile menu is not present. Then clicks the instant_ink_menu_link to expand the parent menu. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are present without clicking them.

Expands the My Account parent menu in the side navigation to reveal its submenu items. First checks if the shipping_billing_submenu_link (a submenu item) is already visible. If not visible, clicks the my_account_menu_link to expand the parent menu. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are present without clicking them.

Waits for the HP Instant Ink menu link to become visible on the page with a very long timeout of 120000 milliseconds (2 minutes). Uses page.wait_for_selector with the instant_ink_menu_link selector string from self.elements and state='visible'. Returns the element locator once it becomes visible. This method is useful for ensuring the side menu has fully loaded before attempting to interact with menu items, particularly useful after page navigation or login.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a sophisticated page object that handles complex menu navigation scenarios including mobile responsive layouts, nested parent-child menu relationships, and automatic menu expansion. The page object demonstrates excellent design patterns with private helper methods (_click_with_parent_expansion, _click_with_instant_ink_expansion, _click_with_account_expansion) that encapsulate retry logic and expansion handling, and public action methods (click_overview, click_update_plan, etc.) that provide simple interfaces for test scripts. The _click_with_instant_ink_expansion method is particularly robust, handling three levels of complexity: direct click, mobile menu expansion, and parent menu expansion. The page sets a default timeout of 30 seconds which is appropriate for dashboard page interactions. All locators use data-testid attributes with descriptive names following the jarvis-react-consumer-layout pattern. The page contains 10 locators and 11 methods (1 constructor, 3 private helpers, 6 public click actions, 2 expand methods, 1 wait method). This page object would be heavily used in any test suite that needs to navigate the dashboard.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Gemini Admin interface. Contains locators for admin page elements including item titles, item content sections, and refund payment functionality. This appears to be an internal admin tool page for managing customer accounts and processing refunds. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates UI elements for the Gemini Admin page, which appears to be an internal administrative interface for HP Instant Ink support or operations teams. Provides locators for viewing item details (titles and content) and performing administrative actions like processing refunds. The page uses a combination of definition list (dl/dt/dd) elements and card-based layout (.card-header, .card-body) to display information. This page object would be used in admin-focused test scenarios or support workflow automation.

locators.ui_element_description: Item title elements on the admin page. Uses a composite selector to match either definition list term elements (dl dt) or card header elements (.card-header). These display the titles or labels for various data items shown on the admin interface.

Item content elements on the admin page. Uses a composite selector to match either definition list description elements (dl dd) or card body elements (.card-body). These display the actual content or values for various data items shown on the admin interface.

Refund payment button or link on the admin page that allows administrators to process payment refunds for customer accounts. This is a child anchor element within a container with class refund_payment_member_link.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the GeminiAdminPage page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance and elements container. Then defines three admin page UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates base_item_title locator using a composite selector 'dl dt, .card-header' to match either definition list terms or card headers, base_item_content locator using composite selector 'dl dd, .card-body' to match either definition list descriptions or card bodies, and refund_payment_button locator using CSS selector '.refund_payment_member_link > a' to target the refund link. The composite selectors allow the page object to work with different HTML structures that may be used in different sections of the admin interface.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a minimal page object with only locators and no action methods defined. It appears to be for an internal admin tool (Gemini) used by HP support or operations teams. The page object only defines 3 locators and relies on BasePageObject for all interaction methods. The locators use composite selectors to handle different HTML structures (definition lists vs card layouts), suggesting the admin interface may have multiple views or sections with different markup patterns. The presence of a refund_payment_button indicates this page is used for financial operations. This page object would likely be extended with action methods as admin workflows are automated. The small size suggests either the page is simple or this is an early/incomplete implementation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP ID authentication and account creation pages. Contains locators for sign-in form elements (username, password, sign-in button), account creation form elements (first name, last name, email, password, country selector, market), email verification elements (code input, submit code button), and helper text elements for form validation. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements for HP ID authentication flows including sign-in, account creation, and email verification. Provides locators for form input fields, submit buttons, validation helper text elements, and page-level containers. This page object handles the HP ID authentication system which is used across HP services. Contains locators for both the sign-in flow (username, password, sign-in button) and the registration flow (first name, last name, email, password, country selector, create account button). Also includes locators for the email verification code entry step and form validation helper text that appears when fields have errors.

locators.ui_element_description: Helper text element that appears below the first name input field to display validation error messages when the first name is invalid or missing.
Helper text element that appears below the last name input field to display validation error messages when the last name is invalid or missing.
Helper text element that appears below the email input field to display validation error messages when the email format is invalid or the email is already registered.
Helper text element that appears below the password input field to display validation error messages when the password does not meet security requirements or is missing.
Create account submit button on the HP ID registration form that submits the new account creation request with the entered user details.
Country selector button within the country selector buttons container. This button opens a dropdown or modal to select the user's country during account creation.
Username or email input field on the HP ID sign-in form where users enter their HP ID email address or username.

Username form submit button that advances to the password entry step after the username has been entered. This is used in multi-step sign-in flows where username and password are entered on separate screens.
Password input field on the HP ID sign-in form where users enter their account password. This is a masked input field of type password.
Sign in submit button on the HP ID sign-in form that submits the authentication request with the entered username and password.

Sign in button option on the HP ID value proposition page. This is a nested selector targeting a sign-in button within the HPID value prop container, likely used on landing pages before the actual sign-in form.
First name input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their first name during registration.
Last name input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their last name during registration.
Email input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their email address which will become their HP ID username.
Market selector dropdown on the HP ID account creation form where users select their geographic market or region.
Submit code button on the email verification page that submits the verification code entered by the user to complete account creation or password reset.
Verification code input field where users enter the numeric or alphanumeric code sent to their email address for account verification or password reset.

Create account button with composite selector that matches either the data-testid attribute or the sign-up ID. This provides fallback selector options for the account creation submit button across different page versions.
HP ID value proposition page container element that displays the benefits and features of creating an HP ID account. This is typically shown before the actual sign-in or registration forms.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the HPIDPage page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance and elements container. Then defines 17 HP ID page UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates locators for form validation helper text elements (firstName_helper_text, lastName_helper_text, email_helper_text, password_helper_text), account creation form fields (firstName, lastName, email, market, country_selector_button, create_account_button, create_account_button_data), sign-in form fields (username, password, sign_in, user_name_form_submit, sign_in_option), email verification fields (code, submit_code), and page container (hpid_value_prop_page). Most locators use ID selectors for reliability, with some using data-testid attributes or composite selectors for flexibility across page variations.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object covers the complete HP ID authentication system including sign-in, registration, and email verification flows. It contains 17 locators but no action methods, relying entirely on BasePageObject for interactions. The locators are well-organized covering three main flows: sign-in (username, password, sign_in, user_name_form_submit), registration (firstName, lastName, email, market, country_selector_button, create_account_button), and verification (code, submit_code). The page includes helper text locators for all form validation messages, which is useful for negative testing scenarios. Some locators have composite selectors (create_account_button_data, sign_in_option) to handle different page versions. The page uses primarily ID selectors which are reliable but may be fragile if IDs change. This is a locator-only page object that would benefit from action methods like perform_sign_in(), create_account(), verify_email_code() to encapsulate common workflows.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP Instant Ink public landing page. Contains comprehensive locators for all landing page sections including header navigation, hero section, how it works section, plan cards, FAQs, footer, country selector, alert banners, enrollment kit banner, paper delivery section, and mobile menu elements. Includes helper methods for accessing plan cards dynamically and property decorators for specific elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the HP Instant Ink public landing page, which is the main marketing and enrollment entry point for the service. Provides extensive locator coverage for all page sections including header with sign-in/sign-up buttons and country picker, hero section with get started button, how it works section with step descriptions, plan section with ink plan cards and navigation arrows, FAQs section with accordion tabs, go green section, refer-a-friend section, paper delivery add-on section, footnotes section, footer with legal links, alert banners, enrollment kit banner, country selector modal, and mobile responsive menu elements. Contains helper methods for dynamically accessing plan cards by index and property decorators for elements that need special handling. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework covering the primary customer-facing landing page.

locators.ui_element_description: Sign in button in the header navigation that navigates to the HP ID sign-in page for existing customers to access their accounts.
Sign up button in the header navigation that initiates the HP Instant Ink enrollment process for new customers.
HP Instant Ink logo in the header that typically links back to the landing page home. Uses partial class match for the logo module.
Plans link in the header navigation that scrolls or navigates to the plans section of the landing page.
Country flag icon in the header that indicates the current country/locale and opens the country selector when clicked.
Hero section container at the top of the landing page that contains the main headline, value proposition, and primary call-to-action button.
Get started button in the hero section that initiates the enrollment process, specifically for the starter kit enrollment flow.
Virtual agent chat button that opens the HP virtual assistant chatbot for customer support.
Fixed position virtual agent button that remains visible as the user scrolls down the page, providing persistent access to the chatbot.
Support section container on the landing page that displays customer support contact information and resources.
Support phone number link in the support section that displays the customer service phone number and may initiate a phone call on mobile devices.
How it works section container that explains the HP Instant Ink service process with step-by-step illustrations and descriptions. This is version 4 of the how it works section design.
Title text for the 'Never run out' step in the how it works section, explaining that HP automatically sends ink before you run out.
Title text for the 'Save time and money' step in the how it works section, explaining the cost savings and convenience benefits.
Plan banner section that may display promotional messaging or important information about available plans.
Plan section container that displays all available HP Instant Ink subscription plans with pricing and features.
Ink plans tab button that switches the view to display ink-only subscription plans.
Landing page plans container that holds all plan cards and plan-related UI elements.
FAQs section container that displays frequently asked questions organized in accordion tabs by category.
Show more button that expands collapsed content sections to reveal additional information, typically used in FAQs or long content areas.
Go green section container that explains the environmental benefits of HP Instant Ink including cartridge recycling and reduced packaging.
Learn more link in the go green section that navigates to detailed information about HP's environmental initiatives.
Refer-a-friend (RAF) section container that explains the referral program where customers can earn rewards by referring friends.
Learn more button in the refer-a-friend section that navigates to detailed information about the referral program terms and rewards.
Footnotes and disclaimers section at the bottom of the landing page that contains legal disclaimers, terms, and footnote references.
Footer section container that contains footer navigation links, legal links, and copyright information. This is version 3 of the global footer design.
HP.com link in the footer that navigates to the main HP corporate website.
Privacy statement link in the footer that navigates to HP's privacy policy document.
Terms of use link in the footer that navigates to the HP Instant Ink terms of service document.
Cookie settings link in the footer that opens the cookie preferences management interface.
Copyright text in the footer displaying HP's copyright notice and year.
Privacy choices link in the footer that navigates to the page where users can manage their privacy preferences and opt-out options.
HP Instant Ink Terms of Service link in the footer that navigates to the specific terms document for the Instant Ink service.
Terms of service page container that displays the full terms of service document content.
Title text for the plan selection step in the how it works section, explaining how to choose a subscription plan.
Title text for the shipping step in the how it works section, explaining how ink cartridges are automatically shipped.
Title text for the flexibility step in the how it works section, explaining the ability to change or cancel plans.
Need more plan info link that opens a modal with additional details about plan features and pricing.
Plans info modal container that displays detailed plan information when the more plans info link is clicked.
Sign in link within the plans info modal that navigates to the sign-in page for existing customers.
Modal close button that dismisses any open modal dialog on the page.
Check printer eligibility link that opens a modal or navigates to a page where users can verify if their printer is compatible with HP Instant Ink.
Image container for the 'Never run out' step in the how it works section version 4.
Image container for the 'Save' step in the how it works section version 4.
When we save section title that introduces the savings comparison section showing cost benefits of HP Instant Ink.
Ink plans tab content container that displays all ink plan cards within the landing page plans container.
Eligibility checker modal title element that appears when users check if their printer is eligible for HP Instant Ink.
Sign up now button on plan cards that initiates the enrollment process for the selected plan.
Refer-a-friend banner message that displays promotional messaging about the referral program.
Refer-a-friend footnote link that provides additional terms and conditions for the referral program.
Right arrow button in the ink plans carousel that navigates to the next set of plan cards.
Left arrow button in the ink plans carousel that navigates to the previous set of plan cards.
Instant Ink for Business section container that promotes the business version of HP Instant Ink for commercial customers.
Paper delivery section container that explains the paper add-on service where customers can have paper delivered along with ink.
Paper delivery section title heading that introduces the paper add-on service.
Paper delivery section description text that explains the benefits and details of the paper add-on service.
Paper delivery section image wrapper containing the visual representation of the paper delivery service.
Learn more about pricing link in the paper delivery section that provides detailed pricing information for the paper add-on.
Superscript footnote reference in the paper delivery section label that links to disclaimer text.
Combined savings footnote link that provides details about how savings are calculated when combining ink and paper services.
Header section container that contains all header navigation elements including logo, menu, sign-in button, and country picker.
Country picker button in the header that opens the country selector modal for changing locale and language.
Manage account button in the header that appears for logged-in users and navigates to the account dashboard.
Sign in link in the mobile hamburger menu that navigates to the sign-in page on mobile devices.
Sign up link in the mobile hamburger menu that initiates enrollment on mobile devices.
How it works link in the mobile hamburger menu that scrolls to the how it works section on mobile devices.
Instant paper add-on link in the mobile hamburger menu that scrolls to the paper delivery section on mobile devices.
Plans link in the mobile hamburger menu that scrolls to the plans section on mobile devices.
FAQs link in the mobile hamburger menu that scrolls to the FAQs section on mobile devices.
Hamburger menu button that opens the mobile navigation menu on smaller screen sizes.
Country selector modal container that displays the list of supported countries when the country picker is clicked.
Country selector modal title that displays the heading for the country selection interface.
Country selector item links that represent individual country options in the country selector modal.
General tab button in the FAQs accordion that displays general frequently asked questions when clicked.
Monthly plans tab button in the FAQs accordion that displays FAQs specific to monthly subscription plans.
Yearly plans tab button in the FAQs accordion that displays FAQs specific to yearly subscription plans.
Pay as you print tab button in the FAQs accordion that displays FAQs specific to pay-as-you-print plans.
Alert banner container that displays important announcements or notifications at the top of the page. This is version 3 of the banner design.
Legacy alert banner container that displays important announcements using the legacy banner module design.
Icon element within the alert banner that visually indicates the type of alert (info, warning, error, success).
Content area within the alert banner that contains the banner message text.
Link element within the alert banner that provides additional information or action related to the alert.
Close button within the alert banner that dismisses the banner when clicked.
Emergency banner header element that displays the title of urgent or critical announcements.
Alert banner title heading that displays the main heading text of the banner message.
Alert banner subtitle that displays secondary heading text below the main banner title.
Alert banner body content that displays the detailed message text of the banner.
Hide/collapse link in the emergency banner that collapses the banner to show only the title.
Show/expand link in the emergency banner that expands the banner to show full content.
Dismiss button in the emergency banner that permanently dismisses the banner for the current session.
Enrollment kit banner container that promotes the HP Instant Ink enrollment kit offer.
Enrollment kit banner content container that holds the banner message and call-to-action.
Enrollment kit banner message container that holds the promotional message text.
Enrollment kit banner message text element that displays the promotional message about the enrollment kit.
Enrollment kit signup link or button that initiates the enrollment process specifically for the enrollment kit offer.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the LandingPage page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance and elements container. Then defines 88 landing page UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. The locators cover all major sections of the landing page including header elements (sign_in_button, sign_up_header_button, header_logo, header_plan, flag_icon, country_picker, manage_account_button, hamburger_menu), hero section (hero_section, get_started_button), support section (support_section, support_phone_number, virtual_agent, virtual_agent_fixed_button), how it works section (how_it_works, how_it_works_never_run_out, how_it_works_save_time_money, how_it_works_plan, how_it_works_ship, how_it_works_flexibility, how_it_works_save, when_we_save), plan section (plan_banner, plan_section, ink_plans_tab, landing_page_plans_container, ink_plans_tab_content, ink_plans_card_right_arrow, ink_plans_card_left_arrow, need_more_plan_info, plans_info_modal, plans_info_modal_signin_link, plan_sign_up_now_button, check_printer_eligibility_link, eligibility_search_modal), FAQs section (faqs_section, show_more_button, faq_general_tab, faq_monthly_plans_tab, This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework with extensive locator coverage for all landing page features.

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card index or identifier to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific ink plan card's pages container by dynamically constructing a selector based on the provided card parameter. Uses page.locator() with an f-string to build the selector '[data-testid="landing-page-plans-container"] [data-testid^="lp-plans-plan-card-{card}"] [class^="styles__PagesContainer-"]' where {card} is replaced with the card parameter value. This allows tests to target specific plan cards by their index or identifier. The locator targets the pages container within a specific plan card, which likely contains pagination or page indicators for multi-page plan details.

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card index or identifier to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific ink plan card by dynamically constructing a selector based on the provided card parameter. Uses page.locator() with an f-string to build the selector '[data-testid="landing-page-plans-container"] [data-testid="lp-plans-plan-card-{card}"] [class^="styles__PagesContainer-"]' where {card} is replaced with the card parameter value. This allows tests to target specific plan cards by their index or identifier for interaction or verification. The locator targets the entire plan card container including all its content.

Property decorator method that returns the first matching flag icon element on the page. Uses page.locator() with the flag_icon selector from self.elements and calls .first to get only the first matching element. This is useful when there are multiple flag icons on the page but you only want to interact with the first one, typically the one in the header. The property decorator allows this to be accessed as an attribute rather than a method call.

Property decorator method that returns the first matching go green section element on the page. Uses page.locator() with the go_green_section selector from self.elements and calls .first to get only the first matching element. This ensures that even if there are multiple elements matching the selector, only the first one is returned. The property decorator allows this to be accessed as an attribute rather than a method call.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most comprehensive page object in the framework with 88 locators covering every section of the HP Instant Ink landing page. The page object demonstrates excellent organization with locators grouped by section (header, hero, how it works, plans, FAQs, footer, etc.). It includes two helper methods (ink_plans_tab_pages and ink_plans_card) that dynamically construct locators for plan cards based on a card parameter, enabling flexible interaction with different plan options. Two property decorators (flag_icon and go_green_section) provide convenient access to specific elements with .first filtering. The page uses a mix of selector strategies including data-testid (most common and most reliable), ID selectors, class selectors with partial matching, and composite selectors. The extensive coverage of alert banners (11 different banner-related locators) suggests robust handling of dynamic notifications. The mobile menu has dedicated locators separate from desktop navigation. The page includes comprehensive footer coverage with 8 footer links. This page object would be central to any landing page test suite and demonstrates the scale and complexity of the public-facing HP Instant Ink website. The locator-only design (minimal action methods) suggests reliance on BasePageObject for interactions or that action methods are defined elsewhere.
